prompt: Check this n8n workflow JSON for issues: [Telegram Echo Bot with wrong prefix x2, chatId plain string, typeVersion 1.1, telegramAPI credential key]
assertions: The output contains the heading '## n8n Workflow Check'
The output identifies both nodes as having the wrong 'nodes-base.' prefix (should be 'n8n-nodes-base.')
The output classifies the incorrect nodeType prefix as an Error, not a Warning
The output identifies chatId on 'Send Reply' as a plain string instead of an __rl object
The output classifies the plain-string chatId as an Error
The output describes or shows the correct __rl format with __rl:true, value, and mode fields
The output reports the typeVersion 1.1 mismatch on Telegram Trigger (expected 1.2)
The output classifies the typeVersion mismatch as a Warning (not an Error)
The output reports the misspelled credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi')
The output offers to apply fixes after presenting the report
The output identifies exactly 4 issues (not more, not fewer)
The output does NOT flag the connections object as having any issues
The output does NOT flag the 'updates: [message]' parameter as wrong
